C: 如山的杂物堆积在办公桌表面，皮皮西人甚至很难从中露出自己的脑袋。
E: Piles of clutter are strewn across the office desk. A Pepeshi person would have found it hard to poke their head through the mess.

C: 为筑梦师们打造的休憩场所。经常有人在这里光明正大地摸鱼。
E: A lounge center built for the Dreamweavers. There are people openly playing hooky in here.

C: 梦泡如虫茧般附在这面屏幕上，使之模糊如锈铁。
E: Dream bubbles are plastered on this screen like insect cocoons, making it blurry as rusted metal.

C: 你看着雅座上空不安分的伞棚，不由想起每当台风天气出行，你与伞是如何艰难地领受大自然的馈赠。
E: You look at the canopy above the private seats and can't help but be reminded of travel during typhoon season — how you and your umbrella braved the ardors of nature's gift.

C: 只是从来没人过问。
E: It's just that no one has ever asked about it.

C: 迤逦灯火幽微，帷幕下影子沉睡……
E: The meandering lights grow faint, the shadows slumber beneath the curtain...

C: 每个人都值得一个机会。对我们而言是这样，对您也是如此！难道您宁可看着财富从指缝间溜走，也不愿稍微做个尝试，把未来紧紧攥在手中吗？
E: Everyone deserves a chance to succeed, and that's true for both you and us! Would you rather see money slipping through your fingers instead of trying to keep it in your grasp?

C: 家里的长辈说，厉害的人能考很多证书，拿到很多很多奖章！所以，我也要收集很多很多很多的证书，还有奖章，来证明我是个厉害的人！
E: My elders tell me that the really smart ones can get certified for a ton of stuff, and get plenty of medals! Which is why I'm also collecting plenty of certificates and medals, to prove that I'm smart too!

C: …看来你完全不明白智械的处境。技术永远在发展，但总有人会被时代丢下。等到哪天，我身上的部件过时了、需要更换了，可市面上却找不到适配的型号该怎么办？
E: ...It appears you don't understand an Intellitron's situation at all. Technology is always advancing, but some will be left behind by the times. When that day comes, the parts within me will be outdated and need to be changed. But what if the market no longer has compatible models available?

C: 是场面描写不够血肉模糊吗？是剧情发展不够惊险刺激吗？是节奏不够紧张、气氛不够压抑吗？
E: Are my descriptions not visceral enough? Is my plot development not harrowing and thrilling enough? Is the pace lacking, the ambience not suppressive enough?

C: 所以说路易斯是■■的傻■吗？！为了个小画家追星追到匹诺康尼，现在好了，塌房了吧？
E: So is this Louis really some ■■ ■ moron or what!? He went all the way to Penacony to pursue some burgeoning painter. And look how it's turned out — it's all gone south now, hasn't it?

C: 要不还是赶紧跑路吧。
E: Maybe we should just scuttle.

C: 汪汪！
E: Woof woof!

C: 真是服了你们这些人类！
E: I really have to hand it to you humans!

C: 说实话，我觉得许多新闻都有…创作的成分，但无伤大雅。
E: To be honest, I think a lot of news reporting has elements of... creativity in it, but it's harmless.

C: 以前不时有观众反馈说，电视台的节目「太老土了」「毫无节目效果」，可惜台里也不重视……
E: We used to have viewers occasionally contact us saying that the stations shows were "outdated" or "not dramatic enough." Unfortunately, the station never took them seriously...

C: 音乐的黄金年代已经彻底结束了，连一点高贵的回响都没剩下。可悲……
E: The golden age of music is completely over. Not even a single notable echo lingers. How tragic...

C: 垃圾桶不发一言，只是听话地跳起了不知名的舞蹈，桶盖转了一圈又一圈。
E: Without a word, the trashcan obediently spins round and round, doing an unknown dance.

C: 这家店是做什么的？
E: What does this store sell?

C: 也许我会以《家人》为题，再画一幅画吧……
E: Maybe I will do another painting under the title, Kinship...

C: 当然可以。您是掌握了什么和「钟表匠」有关的消息了吗？
E: Of course. Have you gained some information on the Watchmaker?

C: 细碎的星震碎片可以成为怀表表盘的点缀，而泯灭帮的淬火可以被用来熔炼座钟的摆锤。根据不同材料的性质，我会将它们打造成最为合适的配件。
E: Fine fragments of starquake can embellish watch faces and the Annihilation Gang's tempering fire can be used to forge a grandfather clock's pendulum. Depending on their properties, I use different materials to make the most fitting parts of a clock.

C: 悬浮在高空中的歌剧院…或许那里就是「钟表匠」的藏身之处？
E: A theater that floats high in the sky... Perhaps that is where the Watchmaker would be hiding?

C: 你还真是没幽默感。罢了罢了，说吧，想吃点什么？
E: Aw, come on, where's your sense of humor? So, what would you like to eat?

C: …我编的段子怎么就不好笑了！不至于十一次笔试都不过吧！
E: ...Are my jokes really not funny? Why did they reject me eleven times!?

C: 如果你和类似的家伙打过交道，应该明白我指的是什么。
E: If you've come across such people, you probably know what I'm talking about.

C: 不过我倒不觉得这是件坏事——即使在梦里，大家也拥有保持愤怒的自由。
E: But I don't think it's a bad thing. Even in dreams, everyone still has the freedom to remain angry.

C: 布罗娜紧皱眉头，怨恨与怒火充盈在她的眼中——如果不赶快劝劝她，下一秒她可能就会把面前的自动乐团砸个稀巴烂。
E: Brona frowns, resentment and hatred filling her eyes — if you don't quickly persuade her, she'll likely smash the automated orchestra in front of her into smithereens in the next second.

C: 现在的受众还能够接受常盘国的二次元，只是因为他们早已习惯了！而并非因为这些真的是全银河最好的动漫产品！
E: The only reason that our audiences are still able to accept Tokiwa anime is because they've long-since grown used to it! It is not because our works are the best in the cosmos at all!

C: 平田平次皱着眉头，但嘴角却挂着微笑。
E: Hirata Heiji furrows his brow, but a smile hangs at the corners of his lips.

C: 很有道理。
E: Fair enough.

C: 应该牢记一个准则——「形式永远服从功能」。只要践行这个准则，功能性自然会带来形式上的美。我还从来没见过任何一个建筑物是「实用但丑陋」的。
E: There is one principle that should be remembered at all times — "Form must always follow function." If you can realize this principle, functionality will naturally bring out the beauty of the form. I have never once seen a building that was "functional but ugly."

C: 她…她又邀请我去音乐咖啡厅了……
E: She... She's invited me to the music café again...

C: 咳、其实我也没有多期待和她共进晚餐，只不过是想去尝尝那家的新品罢了……
E: Ahem, well, it's not that I'm that excited to have dinner with her. I'm just looking forward to trying their new menu...

C: 只能说…那啥，冠军队伍，亦有差距！一群废柴，一场大败！哈哈哈。
E: Let's just say... there was a huge gap between the finalist teams. What a landslide victory! Hahaha!

C: 我还有事，先走了。
E: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

C: …通常而言，我的工作内容不包括陪宾客聊天。但「家族」通常而言不会拒绝宾客的合理请求。说吧，您想聊什么？
E: ...Generally, my duties don't involve chatting with guests, but The Family won't decline reasonable requests. So, what would you like to discuss?

C: 你能提供什么服务？
E: What services do you provide?

C: 好的，如有任何问题，我随时为您效劳。
E: Certainly. I'm always at your service.

C: …真是个精彩的梦境！我愿意从您这里买入！
E: ...What an outstanding dream! I'd love to purchase it!

C: 顺带一提，这可能是你做过的最离奇的梦之一，因为你根本不知道正经的学校长什么样。
E: Interestingly, this dream is one of the most peculiar ones you have ever had, as you have no concept of what a typical school is like.

C: 只要您投入一点点{RUBY_B#信用点}苜蓿币{RUBY_E#}，窥梦电话就能自动检索你的通讯录，找到您正在熟睡的某位朋友——
E: Just insert a small amount of {RUBY_B#credits}Alfalfa credits{RUBY_E#}, and the phone will automatically search your contacts for a sleeping friend and allow you to listen to their dreams...

C: 有什么推荐的吗？
E: Do you have any recommendations?

C: 有其他办法吗？
E: Is there anything else I can do besides charging?

C: 你和湛蓝爵士面面相觑。它似乎没有要开口的意思。
E: You come face-to-face with Dr. Blues, but it does not look like it's going to speak anytime soon.

C: 我完成小鸟们的委托了！
E: I've completed the commission for the birdies!

C: 有问题就憋着，什么都好奇只会害了你。
E: Keep your problems to yourself. Curiosity will only kill you.

C: 太棒了，快把奖品拿来。
E: Sounds fabulous. Get me my prize now.

C: 我想想…对大多数人来说，这片美梦应该是当之无愧的天堂。
E: Let me think... For the majority of people, this wonderful dream would be an absolute paradise.

C: 听着越来越离谱了…
E: This list just gets weirder and weirder...

C: 我曾多次向心中的邪念屈服，做出违反道德的事情，包括但不限于涂改留言、寄回作业、打捞喷泉……
E: Many times, I gave in to my evil thoughts, performing deeds that were immoral, including but not limited to altering messages, sending back homework, salvaging coins from fountains...

C: 曾经跟我一起追剧的好友们早就退坑了。大家都说自己有很多事情要忙，不能再把时间花在这些没用的东西上。
E: The friends who used to binge-watch the series with me quit way early on. Everyone says they've got a lot of stuff to do, and have no time to spend on such useless activities.

C: 你又来啦…所以，愿意花些时间听听我的烦恼吗？
E: You're back... So, willing to spend some time to listen to my woes?

C: 哈哈，并不是，我甚至没怎么看过动画。但不得不说，他和钟表小子之间纯粹的友谊令我非常羡慕。
E: Haha, not really. I actually barely watched the animations. But still, I find the pure friendship between Clockie and him very enviable.

C: 如果你不介意的话，我们可以聊一些别的？
E: How about we talk about something else if you don't mind?

C: 他究竟为什么…会变成这副样子呢？
E: Just why... did he turn out this way?

C: 我承认他在「梦与心灵」领域的研究无人可及，但这不意味着他能成为一个独裁者、能掌握对思想和话语的生杀大权！
E: Indeed, his research in the field of dreams and psychology was unparalleled, but that didn't give him the right to be a tyrant! Or to decide whether some thoughts and speech should exist!

C: 皮皮西的嘴角浮现起一抹微笑，这使得他看上去更加年轻、也更有活力了一些。
E: The Pepeshi breaks into a faint grin that makes him appear more youthful and vibrant.

C: 有次我离开公馆去「黄金的时刻」采购，在路边遇到了一个带货的逐梦客。
E: There was a time I went out to buy something in the Golden Hour. I bumped into a Dreamchaser who worked as a salesman along the way.

C: 可是你身上什么都没有…
E: But I can't see you wearing anything...

C: 你如何看待美梦？
E: What are your thoughts on dreams?

C: 您可以在此处与扮演它们的皮套演员互动，并游玩与各个卡通形象相关的游艺项目。
E: You can interact with costumed performers who are dressed as cartoon characters and enjoy all sorts of entertainment based on these characters.

C: 哎呦，怎么会有这么缺心眼的家伙啊——随便施舍点小恩小惠就对你感恩戴德，真好打发。
E: Ah, how could someone be so slow! Give him a little bit of charity and he's kissing your feet! He's so easy to please.

C: 一尊平平无奇的隐夜鸫雕像，并不承载任何明显或隐含的寓意及宣示意图。
E: An unremarkable Nightingale Statue that illustrates no obvious or hidden propaganda or intentions.

C: …你接的太烂了。
E: ...You're terrible at this.

C: 我想想…嗯，要不干脆转行去演话剧好了，老是讲脱口秀确实没啥前途啊。
E: Let me think... Hmm, maybe I should change careers and start acting? There really isn't much future in being a stand-up comedy host, after all...

C: 我回去问问皮皮西俱乐部的管理员，看他们想不想来这团建。找个能让所有会员都满意的地方挺不容易的，他们不喜欢外人多的场所。
E: I'll go and ask the Pepeshi Club administrator if they want to come here for a team-building event. It's not easy to find a place that everyone is happy with. They don't like places with lots of outsiders.

C: 您稍等，我这就来准备。
E: Just a moment. It'll start soon.
(Meow...)

C: 当然。见人说人话，见鬼说鬼话，这是猎犬特工的必备素质。
E: Of course not. Changing your personality to fit the person you're with is a trait a Hound agent must have.

C: 聊点什么好呢…啊，有了——听说过「安谧的时刻」么？有机会的话，我可以带你去逛逛。参观参观噩梦边境的监牢，见识各种有趣的…人。
E: What else could we talk about... Ah, I know. Have you heard of the Moment of Serenity? If we have the opportunity, I can take you to check it out. You can see the jail on the Nightmare's edge and meet some interesting... people.

C: 省吃俭用，辛勤劳动，成功就在我的前方招手。
E: If I live frugally and work hard, success will be just around the corner.

C: 欧利马的委屈涌上心头，愤怒的情绪重新泵入欧利马的血管，仿佛下一刻就会嘭地炸开。
E: Olma's grievances well up in her heart, and anger pumps into her blood vessels once again. She seems like she might explode.

C: 至于发生了什么，想必你比我更清楚。鸢尾花家系现在正在重新选拔调弦师…虽然他们都觉得知更鸟小姐还会重返舞台，但我不这么认为。
E: You know better than I do as to what happened. The Iris Family is now auditioning for another Chordmaster... and despite them all thinking Miss Robin is ready to make a return to the stage, I beg to differ.

C: 没错，托斯卡尼就是我——我是这个乐团的总指挥。
E: That's right. I'm Toskani — and I'm the conductor of this orchestra.

C: 整个匹诺康尼的通信网络都在我的掌控之中，想篡改你账户里的数据对我来说轻而易举——我的权能超乎你的想象，别逼我出手。
E: I have all of Penacony's communication network in the palm of my hand, and changing your account data is a piece of cake for someone like me — my powers are far greater than you could ever imagine. Don't force my hand.

C: 哎呀，这个说来话长。跟大部分人差不多，眼睛一黑，醒来就被带到这了。
E: Ah, that's a long story. Much like the majority of people here, I blacked out and woke up here.

C: 停止试探。
E: Give up on speaking to him.

C: 唉，这样的日子还能持续多久呢？
E: Ah, how much longer can the days continue like this?

C: 啧…我只是想体验男耕女织的简单生活，为什么就这么难呢！
E: Tsk... I just want to experience a simple, traditional life. Why should that be so hard!?

C: 例如逻辑、数学这样的课程…是的，它们能极大地提升思维能力。
E: You mean like logic and mathematics...? You're right. That will help them to greatly improve their thinking skills.

C: 如果你对历史有兴趣的话，我可以为你讲上一天一夜。
E: If you're interested in history, I can talk with you all day and night.

C: （拿出一枚艾迪恩代币）
E: (Take out an Aideen Token)

C: 小鸟矜持地叼过你拿出的代币，然后…吃掉了它！
E: The bird gingerly picks up the coin you give it, and... swallows it!

C: 嘿呀！何等硬派的速度，逃跑的小家伙们竟然全给你抓到了啾。
E: Hee-ya! You've got some hardcore speed! The escaped little fellas have all been caught by you, tweet.

C: 你是诗人吗？
E: Are you a poet?

C: 这原初的梦境，蕴藏着无限可能性。在这里，错误的得以被纠正，缺失的能够被补充……
E: This Primitive Dreamscape contains infinite possibilities. Here, mistakes can be corrected, and inadequacies can be patched up...

C: 不打扰你了。
E: I'll leave you to it.

C: 我问你一个问题——知道「哈努努」这个名字吗？
E: Let me ask you a question: Do you know the name "Hanunue"?

C: 可以聊聊吗？
E: Are you up for a chat?

C: 机会难得，得好好规划才行……
E: Since this is such a rare opportunity, I must come up with a detailed plan...

C: 在这方面您又保守了起来啊，毕竟…草莓无论怎么搭配，味道都会相当亮眼。
E: In this aspect, you've made another conservative choice. After all... strawberries have a distinct flavor, no matter what they're paired with.

C: 又或者，喜欢看我们身旁的云气，被这艘船轻轻一撞，就得消去无痕？
E: Or maybe you enjoy watching the clouds disperse without a trace after we bump into them?

C: 而在这十五分钟里，除了留下后代之外，它们所有的时间都用以积蓄力量，来产生会将自己杀死的一闪而过的光点。
E: In those <unbreak>15</unbreak> minutes, apart from assuring the continuation of their species, they spend the rest of their time collecting the energy required to create a flash of light — the same light that would also end their lives.

C: 温控是牛排的命门，因此绝不提供星际快送。上门的食客络绎不绝，预约更是排到三个琥珀纪之后。」
E: "The steak's quality hinges on precise temperature control, so interstellar delivery isn't offered. Such is its demand that reservations are booked up to three Amber Eras ahead."

C: 当你手头有大把花不完的钞票，全银河最昂贵的牛排就是美食。
E: When you have so much wealth you can't spend it all, the most expensive steak in the cosmos becomes gourmet.

C: 后来我找了一群老乡，做起了走私甲虫的生意。很快这种甲虫热销银河，我也靠这生意狠狠赚了一笔。
E: I got a bunch of locals together and started a beetle smuggling business. Our business rapidly boomed across the Cosmos and money started rolling in.

C: 唉…算了，我总是装不像。
E: *Sigh* Never mind. I can't ever seem to fake it right.

C: 还有托马斯…说是开音乐公司是为了捧我，可从装修到制作音乐的钱，每一笔款项都有问题…在他入狱之前，我真的能保全自己无罪脱身吗？
E: Then there's Thomas. He says the music studio is for my career, but every payment, from renovations to production, is rigged. Can I clear myself of the implications before he lands in jail?

C: 哎呀呀，你又不是小孩子，就别搞什么「人气排行榜」那一套了。名气这种东西，能拿来变现才是最重要的～
E: Oh, come on, are you a kid? Quit making this into a popularity contest. What really matters is using your fame to make money!

C: 度蜜月也要出差，这么拼？
E: Working even during your honeymoon? You're a workaholic, huh?

C: 我的老朋友曾经这么评价过我：「麦吉尔，你在法庭上就像是只猫，总是灵活又冷静。」这就是猫科动物为什么杀伤力如此之大。
E: An old friend once said this about me, "McGill, you're so calm and quick on your feet in court, almost like the human embodiment of a cat." This is also why felines are so deadly, by the way.

C: 让我想想…对了，有个严肃的孩子非常注重纪律，或许正在哪里<color=#dbc291ff>监督人们排队</color>呢啾~
E: Let me think... Oh right, there's a serious child who's zealous about discipline. Perhaps it is <color=#dbc291ff>watching over people queuing</color> somewhere, tweet~

C: 顶着我的画像，生意怎么样？
E: How's business after using my portrait?

C: 好的！
E: Okay!

C: 真的很抱歉。我刚学调饮没多久，怕技艺不熟练，给客人调饮时出错，所以提前练习一下道歉的话术。
E: I'm really sorry. I just got into drinksmithing and I doubt I'm good enough yet... I'm rehearsing my apologies in case I slip up and serve badly mixed drinks to the customers...

C: 集中注意力，别让我发现你们中有人在偷懒！记住，在这艘空艇上，每个人都必须全力以赴，包括我在内！从现在起，我希望看到的是效率和纪律。
E: Stay focused, and don't let me catch any of you slacking off! Remember that everyone on this ship, including me, must give their all! From now on, I want you to show me efficiency and discipline.

C: 嘴巴还挺甜的…
E: You say such nice things...

C: 船长您来了！孩子们都过来，跟船长问个好！
E: Captain, you're here! Children, come over and greet the captain!

C: 不敢当不敢当，我只是多上了几年学而已，差不多半个琥珀纪吧。
E: Oh no, of course not. I'm just a student who went to school for a few years more than the others — almost half an Amber Era.

C: （希望承接恋爱咨询业务）
E: (Tell him your interest in starting a love consultancy.)

C: 商机无处不在，而我能听见它们的声音。
E: Business opportunities are around every corner, and I can hear them calling out to me.

C: 唉，身为学生会主席，我已经向学校提过无数次，要扩大实习经历在绩点中的占比，却被当成是欺负贫困学生而数次被警告。
E: *sigh* I'm only doing my job as president of the student council, submitting suggestion after suggestion to increase the weightage of the internship module in our overall GPA, but all I get in return are warnings from the university! For discriminating against students from less well-off backgrounds!

C: 真是荒谬…如果有人问我推荐哪门课，我一定要举个大牌子告诉他：「快逃」！
E: As if they aren't absurd enough already... If anyone comes asking for my recommendation on which classes to take, you bet I'll wave a giant "DROP OUT NOW" placard in their face!

C: 说说财富学院？
E: Tell me about Fortune Academy?

C: 别误会了，我愿意配合他们的演出，更要感谢他们把我推到聚光灯下——
E: Don't get me wrong. I am happy to play along with their little performance. I'm actually grateful for them pushing me into the spotlight...

C: 没什么问题。
E: I don't have any more questions.

C: 但…这里是匹诺康尼，梦境里的人们大多身世显赫，对地位与价值更在乎，也更敏感。
E: But... this is Penacony. The majority of people from the Dreamscape are from notable backgrounds and are far more interested in and wary of status and value.

C: 只有这样，我才能留在这里，从无数学生中脱颖而出，有机会实现自己的梦想。
E: That is the only way that I will be able to stay here, stand out amongst the countless other students, and have a chance to achieve my dreams.

C: （继续旁观）
E: (Keep observing)

C: 要我说，追求独立创作也好，迎合商业潮流也罢，说到底，不过是在生存和理想间做出选择罢了。谁能说自己的选择就一定是对的呢？
E: If you ask me, it doesn't matter if you're making independent works or going with the commercial trends. At the end of the day, you're doing nothing more than making a decision between survival and your ideals. Who can say that one choice is ultimately more correct?

C: 那你后续有什么打算？
E: So what's next for you?

C: 再说…我也想过，他们的水平都很高，自己是否真的能承担起乱破忍首的职责，帮他们实现各自的忍道呢？我…没这个信心。
E: Moreover... I've also thought about it — they are very good. Can I really pick up the mantle of Head Ninja Rappa's role and help them fulfill their own way of the ninja? I... don't have that kind of confidence.

C: 为什么？因为这两个衡量标准都是假的，没法用数字量化，而数字不会骗人。
E: Why? Because these two standards of measurement are unquantifiable and therefore false. Numbers do not lie.

C: 但既然学生都喜欢，观众也表示认可，那行吧，姑且表扬下你们也不是不可以。
E: But if the students like it and the audience also approves, then that's fine. I'm not above giving you a compliment or two.

C: 嘿嘿，以前我总觉得自己家境不如其他同学好，读书也读不明白，一无是处…现在我感觉其实我也没那么差嘛。
E: Hee-hee. I used to feel like my family background wasn't as good as the others, and I'm not great at studying either. I felt useless... but now, I feel like I'm not so bad after all.

C: 「你有什么好怕的！我才该害怕！等一下我还要一个人走回来呢！」
E: "What do you have to be afraid of? I should be the one afraid! I have to walk back alone later!"

C: 想知道你的过去…
E: I want to know more about your past...

C: 现有的信息…
E: The current information we have...

C: 在悲剧中嚎哭的人偶，高举的手得不到救赎。
E: A puppet who cries in a tragedy, with hands raised for unreachable salvation.

C: 本节目由苏乐达独家冠名赞助播出，苏乐达，您观赛娱乐的不二之选。
E: This program is brought to you by SoulGlad, our exclusive sponsor. SoulGlad, the best choice for watching the auditions.

C: 监控镜头缩放到极限，你好像看到了某些高耸入云建筑，前方是「黄金的时刻」吗？你无法辨认出那儿究竟是哪里。
E: Zooming in as far as you can, you seem to be able to make out a few towering buildings. Is that the Golden Hour? You cannot tell for sure what the place ahead is.

C: …奇怪，这雕像是什么时候摆的，昨天路过的时候这里好像还是空的。
E: ...That's strange, when was this statue placed? I remember this place being empty when I passed by yesterday.

C: 会是这里吗？这个以喇叭墙饰为遮掩的玩意儿——你打开看了看
E: Could this be it? This thing that's hidden by the wall decoration of a horn — you open it to take a look.

C: 我想长高一点…
E: I wish I were a little bit taller...

C: 你太难被注意到了。
E: It's pretty hard to notice you.

C: 这下面有点阴冷。
E: It's a bit chilly and damp down here.

C: 我可是个大人物！
E: I'm a big shot!

C: 为什么退出了呢？
E: Then why did you leave?

C: 当我没问…
E: Forget I said anything...

C: …还用得着在这儿当保安？
E: ...Do you think I'll still be here working as a security guard?

C: 是《班尼古堡》吧？
E: It's Benny's Castle, no?

C: 现在造个梦可真难…
E: Building a dream is so difficult nowadays...

C: …智械魅力时刻？
E: ...A moment of Intellitron charming?

C: 这是专门给车用的…
E: This is exclusive to vehicles...

C: 总有些人在现实就活得匆匆，到了梦里也急吼吼的，不知道为了什么……
E: There are always people who rush through life in reality and even in the Dreamscape. I wonder why...

C: 等一会儿。
E: Hold on for a moment.

C: 不错，确实该好好休息一下。我都想好了，等到完成手头的工作，我就回一趟老家，和未婚妻结……
E: Sounds great. It's about time to take a break. As for me, once this project is done, I'll go back to my hometown and get married....

C: 咳，要是您方便的话…
E: Ahem, if it's okay with you...

C: 我也去看看。
E: I'll check it out, too!

C: 请时刻注意人身安全。
E: Please be aware of your safety at all times.

C: 演出均已延期…
E: Performances are postponed...

C: 她的每句歌词我都能背下来。
E: I memorized every single line from her songs.

C: 谁知道你这么不争气——考题都甩你脸上了，结果你看看自己演的都是什么，笑比哭还难看，就那么两句台词还能念错。
E: Who knew you'd be such a disappointment. The test was a freebie, but just look at your performance — your smile was worse than your weeping, and you even flubbed the only two lines you had.

C: 就让匹诺康尼见识见识我们二人的实力吧！
E: Let Penacony bear witness to the strength we wield together!

C: 两个半道杀出的选手，居然一路打到了最后，真是出乎意料。
E: Isn't it crazy how those two participants, who seemed to come out of nowhere, ended up making it to the very end?

C: 哎，咱俩的房间好像触发什么装置了，床垫都湿透了，现在还没收拾好…搞什么……
E: Hey, our room seems to have triggered some device. The bed's sopping wet, and they haven't finished cleaning it yet... What's going on...

C: 恭喜两位啊，我订好了餐厅，我们可以边吃边聊。
E: Congratulations to both of you. I've booked a restaurant, and we can chat while we eat.

C: …好像是有这么些风声。但你觉不觉得好突然，有点不真实了。
E: ...Yes, there are indeed rumors about it, but don't you think it's too sudden? Like, it's not very real.

C: 不嘛，小气鬼！
E: No! You're so stingy!

C: 我不会还在做梦吧…
E: I'm not still dreaming, am I...

C: 这船怎么就易主了？
E: Why is the ship changing hands?

C: 嘘！有人来了！
E: Shhh! Someone's coming!

C: 一切为了，家人！！！
E: All for my family!!!

C: 你们两个，要不要转来财富学院？
E: You two, do you want to transfer to Fortune Academy?

C: 藿藿！听说绥园里又冒出来好多鬼火！我们再去探险吧。
E: Huohuo! I heard that heliobi are haunting Fyxestroll Garden again! We should go check it out.

C: 只要您投入一点点{RUBY_B#信用点}苜蓿币{RUBY_E#}，窥梦电话就能自动检索你的通讯录，找到您正在熟睡的某位朋友——
E: Just insert a small amount of {RUBY_B#credits}Alfalfa credits{RUBY_E#}, and the phone will automatically search your contacts for a sleeping friend and allow you to listen to their dreams...

C: 叮铃铃，「讲道德的匹诺康尼派对客」——我得承认，您的笑话确实很有品！
E: *ring* An "ethical partygoer in Penacony"! I must admit that your joke is quite refined!

C: 眼前的少女一动不动，双眼空洞，平等地无视周遭的一切，这份超然在喧闹的市集中显得有些格格不入……
E: The young woman before you sits frozen, her gaze vacant, seemingly detached from the bustling city around her, lost in a world of her own as if she is surreal...

C: …周边无老哥出没，危机解除！所有事情记得替我保密呀。
E: ...Brother alarm lifted! Remember, what I say is just between us.

C: …昨天到底怎么回事，你为什么没去？
E: ...What happened yesterday? Why didn't you show up?

C: 够了够了！你是不是脑子有病啊？都说了我对这些玩意没兴趣！等等——
E: Stop it! I've had enough! Are you nuts or what? I already told you I'm not interested! Oh, I see...

C: 你尝尝这个、品品那个，囫囵地将拼盘里的菜肴拌在一起吃下——
E: Trying the ingredients in a haphazard order, you eventually mix everything on the platter and consume it all...

C: 你砸吧砸吧嘴，半晌才从这股怪味中回过神来。而此时你才后知后觉地品尝到其中极为浅淡的葡萄味。
E: It hits your taste buds hard, leaving you stunned by the unexpected flavor. Only later do you catch a very faint hint of grape in the aftertaste.

C: …又是昏迷？
E: ...I passed out, again?

C: 瞧吧，就说不用担心，塔兰顿说了：「危机如鹰隼时时盘旋于顶，机警如狡狐引其脱出绝境」——只要多留心，总归不会有什么大问题。
E: See? Told you not to worry. Remember what Talanton teaches us, "Though danger may swoop like an eagle, let your vigilance guide you like a clever fox." Stay alert, and you'll be just fine.

C: 石榴啊…这种水果可是悬锋人的最爱。据说在他们的传统中，石榴汁因为色泽好似鲜血，就被视作可以补气回血、活络身心的灵药呢。
E: Pomegranate... that's the favorite fruit for Kremnoans. Pomegranate juice, with its blood-like color, is seen as a revitalizing elixir for both body and mind in their customs.

C: 只消一小口，唇齿暗自生香；又一口，喉舌余韵回甘；再一口，脏腑如沐清泉。
E: A small nip reveals a deep fragrance that envelops your lips and tongue. Another nip leaves a sweet lingering aftertaste. Another nip refreshes your innards like a cool spring.

C: 厨师也很伟大。和卫兵一样，都能让人，身体健康。
E: But cooking is important too. Like guards, cooks help keep people alive and healthy.

C: 不过，你也不用太担心；只要黄金裔还打算将泰坦取而代之，只要普通人里还有像我这样的冒牌信徒，奥赫玛的气氛就还不至于难以忍受…如果他们声称自己信的是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}就更好了！
E: But hey, don't stress too much. As long as there are Chrysos Heirs aiming to overthrow and become Titans, and there are still ordinary people like me pretending to be devout, Okhema's vibe won't be too suffocating... especially if they claim to worship {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}.

C: 真正的祭司，比如缇宝大人她们，那我是相当敬重的，也认可他们是人们的组织者——我只是觉得，这样就够了，节外生枝搞迷信实在是累人累己。
E: I have respect for genuine priests like lady Tribbie and the others, and I acknowledge them as leaders of humanity — but that's enough for me. All that extra superstitious stuff is so exhausting.

C: 咦？你对研究感兴趣？真的？你要是唠这个，我可不困了啊。
E: Oh, really? You're interested in my research? Well, in that case, I'm all fired up.

C: 您这个表情…难道是见到扎格列斯本尊了？之前每一位买下来的客人都和您差不多反应…真是太令人好奇了。
E: That face of yours... Did you meet Zagreus in person? Every customer who bought this reacted similarly to you... How curious.

C: 都是熟人了，不问了。
E: I know everyone well, and there's nothing more I need to know.

C: 与阁下的交流十分愉快，我很期待再度与你畅谈。
E: It's been an absolute delight to speak with you, {F#Lady}{M#Lord} {NICKNAME}. I look forward to our next talk.

C: 你的占卜准吗？
E: Are they accurate?

C: 你…算了，我和这么个神婆计较什么…喂，他们都说你占卜得很准，那就给我看看最近的运势如何吧。
E: You... Forget it. Why am I arguing with a diviner hag... Hey, they all say that your divinations are super accurate. If that's the case, tell me what my fortune is like.

C: 当然啦。热热闹闹的人群，要是像这样从远处看，那还是很不错的…但你要是靠近乃至参与进去，一切就会瞬间变得无聊透顶。
E: Course I do. The thronging crowds are fantastic to watch from a distance like this... But once you draw closer or even join in, everything becomes utterly boring in an instant.

C: 哎，你们这些年轻人是有所不知了，被黑潮所影响到的可不仅仅是我们和那些动物。
E: *sigh* You youngsters don't even know! It's not just us and the creatures that are impacted by the black tide.

C: 我看你的身材，像个战士。需要我传授些保命的技巧吗？
E: You have the body of a warrior. Would you like me to teach you some tricks to protect yourself?

C: 但说无妨。
E: There's no harm in asking.

C: 好，接下来是中场休息时间。
E: All right, it's time for a break anyway.

C: 魔法…是什么？工艺，还是原料？
E: Magic... what is it? Craft, or raw material, is it?

C: 我们前往海岸，取海水作为基底，盛入特殊的容器中。一日中不同时段的海水能为秘酿带来不同的风味——
E: We head to the coast, collect seawater to create its base, then pour it into a special vessel. Seawater collected at different times of the day applies different flavors to the ambrosia...

C: 光源飘来了甜美的歌声，让人忆起海风的自然之声，又带有人性的逗弄与撩拨，随它飘荡或缓缓地掩沉。你选择——
E: Sweet song trails into the light source, reminiscent of nature's sea breeze, bringing with it the laughter and allure of humans, drifting with it or slowly sinking. You choose to...

C: 你不是个探险家嘛？
E: Aren't you an explorer?

C: 可我证明了，「黎明机器」的光线会有变化。那是不是…我们就可以依据这个来制定新的历法了？是不是我，达米亚诺斯，修正了刻法勒的创造？！
E: But I have proven that the light from the Dawn Device does indeed change. Could we then base a new calendar on this discovery? Am I, Damionis, actually correcting Kephale's original creation?

C: 假使有位好事的吟游诗人，在诗歌中虚构了某座城邦，并借助自己的名声让所有人都相信，这就是远古曾存在过的地点，只不过现在被摧毁了。
E: What if there were a great bard that created a fictional city in their songs and used their fame to convince everyone that it really existed, and it was just destroyed long ago?

C: 可不是嘛，太吓人了！前几天传信石版上有人说，有个外地人在客房里蹲了两星期没出门，把自己活活饿死了……
E: Absolutely. Terrifying, isn't it! Just the other day, someone on the teleslate mentioned an outlander who holed up in a hotel room for two weeks without leaving and starved to death.

C: 结果回到墓地那边一看，也就几分钟的功夫，那些不三不四的年轻人竟然完全不见了踪影！地上也没有任何脚印，仿佛一切都没有发生过一样……
E: When they got back to the graveyard just a few moments later, the rowdy teens had mysteriously vanished without a trace! Not a single footprint remained, as if the entire episode had been an illusion...

C: 在以前，翁法罗斯还很和平的时代，悬锋城会定期举办祭典，健身也是人气很高的竞技大类呢，听说优胜者会受到贵族女子的青睐呢。
E: In the peaceful days of old, Amphoreus used to hold regular festivals in Castrum Kremnos, where fitness competitions were also a popular event. I heard that the winners would even gain the favor of aristocratic ladies.

C: 好，好，真是个亲切的孩子。放心，不会占用你太多时间。
E: Ah, good. You're such a friendly kid. Don't worry, it won't take up too much of your time.

C: 对呀——所以剩下的你们得自己去调查啊。难不成还要我把前因后果掰开揉碎了给你们讲清楚？那可是另外的价钱了哦。
E: Exactly! Which is why you'll have to figure out the rest yourselves. Or do you expect me to break it all down and explain every detail? That would cost you extra, you know.

C: 后来我姐姐纠正我，他是在损我。可我至今仍不明白，为什么夸我帅是一种咒骂？
E: Later, my older sister corrected me, saying that he was insulting me. But to this day, I still don't understand — how is calling me handsome an insult?

C: 所以你是在划水吧…
E: So you're just slacking off, huh...

C: 你好，善良的听众。希望我演奏的音乐，能为你疲惫的心灵带来些许宁静。
E: Hello, kind listener. I hope my music will ease your fatigued spirits.

C: 向您问好，善良的听众。您聆听这乐声也有段时间了，有什么特别的感受吗？
E: Greetings, kind listener. You have been listening to this music for quite a while. Do you feel anything special?

C: 简而言之，很久以前，{RUBY_B#最初的学者}塞勒苏斯{RUBY_E#}在{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}裂分之枝，瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的神躯栖居之处，带领众建立起了花园与庭院。
E: In short, a long time ago, the {RUBY_B#First Scholar}Thalesus{RUBY_E#} led the masses and built a garden and grove over where the {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Bough of Rift, Cerces'{RUBY_E#} divine shell laid.

C: 总之，尼卡多利品尝了那带有神力的佳酿，感到十分满意，便以蜜酿的制作工艺为条件，收回了对法吉娜城邦的惩戒。
E: Anyway, Nikador had a sip of this fine brew imbued with divinity, expressed utter satisfaction, and rescinded the punishment on Phagousa's city, with knowledge of the honey brew's production as a condition.

C: 离开之前，我顺手牵走了三头角猪，那天晚上的全肉宴是我这辈子最愉快的一顿饭。我喂饱了许多人，也救了许多人。
E: Before I left, I hauled three hornswine by the reins out of their camp. That night at the all-meat buffet was the most fulfilling meal of my life. I fed a whole bunch of people, and saved them too.

C: 哼，神秘商品——法吉娜的圣兽。拿去吧，好好放在你的房间里。
E: Hmph. A mysterious item... the sacred beast of Phagousa. You should take it and put it on display in your room.

C: 这究竟是人类的客套话，还是魔阴身发作的前兆？真是搞不懂你们人类。
E: Is this typical human politeness, or a sign of being mara-struck? Humans are truly baffling.

C: 不过家里的生活真是惬意啊，有可口的饭菜，温暖的地髓炉，相亲相爱的家人。即使一天什么都不做，关是躺在椅子上看报纸就很好。
E: Life at home is truly blissful, complete with delicious meals, the warmth of a Geomarrow furnace, and loving family members. Even just lounging in a chair and reading the newspaper all day feels perfect.

C: 你跟塔米拉关系好吗？
E: Are you close with Tamila?

C: …哈哈，虽然这样的说法着实有些失礼，但我能感受到您的善意，所以…非常感谢。
E: ...Haha. That turn of phrase seems a little disrespectful but, I can sense your sincerity and kindness... Thank you so much.

C: 说得更明白些…阁下，若你有心求取神谕，也总该向三相神殿表露些诚意吧？
E: Let me make it clearer for you... My Lord, if you wish to obtain your prophecy, you must show your piety to the Temple of the Three Fates. Don't you agree?

C: 哎，果然你也不理解。劝你别去揭发，只会让那些卫兵白白收笔贿赂。
E: Ah, as expected, you don't understand me either. Trust me. Don't bother reporting it. It'll just end with the guards pocketing bribes for nothing.

C: 话说回来，你要是不买，我们可以互相行个方便。但如果有感兴趣的，我们就再聊聊？
E: Anyway, if you're not buying, maybe we can both excuse ourselves. But if you're interested, we can keep talking.

C: （讲讲缇宝吧）
E: (Tell him about Tribbie)

C: 比初印象要有趣、可亲得多。
E: You're much more approachable and interesting than my first impression of you.

C: …小灰，吓*我们*一跳。但门径好像没打开诶……
E: ...Little Gray, you scared us! And it doesn't seem that the gate is opened...

C: 想象不到，像个梦境。
E: Just can't picture that. It feels like a dream.

C: 那您的善恶标准还挺松弛的。
E: Your standards for good and bad seem... flexible.

C: …你现在才发现吗？
E: ...You realized just now?

C: 奥赫玛城曾有诗人在生前说，遗言是「在一息尚存之时，用充满睿智的话语华丽地将自己送达{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的身边。」
E: A poet from Okhema once said that last words are "words of wisdom, uttered with a lingering breath, that deliver oneself gracefully to {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos'{RUBY_E#}s side."

C: 我还记得一些逝者的*墓志铭*，如果你感兴趣的话，我可以告诉你。
E: I still recall some of the deceased's epitaph. I can tell you if you're curious.

C: 打扰了，我只是路过。
E: Excuse me, I'm just passing through.

C: 年轻人，不敬泰坦可没有好果子吃！
E: Young one, you'll regret not paying the Titan due respect!

C: 看看这些圣像、这些祭坛…{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}始终庇护着他虔诚的子民，连凯妮斯元老也常来此地祈祷。她深知敬畏之心的重要。
E: Look at the sacred statues and these altars... {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} has always watched over their devout followers. Even Elder Caenis frequents this place for her prayers. She understands the importance of reverence.

C: …那我只能继续忘着了。
E: ...Then I'll just keep forgetting.

C: 我同意。回想起阿那克萨戈拉斯的那番言论，结局或许比单纯的死亡还要糟糕。
E: I agree. Recalling Anaxagoras's words, the outcome might be worse than simply death.

C: （继续听）
E: (Keep listening.)

C: 失乡人啊，去寻觅末世的净土吧\n别放弃火焰、清泉和丰壤
E: O displaced souls, go seek the pure land at the end of the world.\nDon't give up on the flames, the fresh waters, and the fertile soil.

C: 但是目前来看，那份来自天空的祝福并未离去，甚至…更加温暖。
E: But it seems the sky's blessing hasn't left us. In fact, it feels... even warmer now.

C: 你果真了解我，比起担忧这条命，我更牵挂那些岁月的见证。
E: You really do know me. Instead of worrying about my life, I care more about those testimonies of times lived.

C: （连这么小的孩子都……！）
E: (They won't spare even a little kid...!)

C: 嗯…有位织者沉迷于浴池带来的乐趣。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>浴场的中央水池</color>中，不知骚乱是否打扰了它的雅兴？
E: Hmm... There is a weaver absorbed in the pleasure brought by the bath. The golden thread brings tidings: They are at <color=#dbc291ff>the central pool of the baths</color>. I wonder if the chaos has interrupted their enjoyment?

C: 稍微？几节？
E: A few classes, huh?

C: 对了，你要是想学怎么逮田鼠，可以找他教你。那小子技术可好了。
E: By the way, if you wanna learn how to catch field mice, he's the one to ask. That kid's got real skills.

C: 你能想象吗？虽说只有个外形，但有的孩子已经能拿木头削个小木马出来了。哈哈，说不定很有当雅努斯祭司的潜质呢。
E: Can you believe it? Though it's just a rough shape, some kids can already carve little wooden horses by hand. Hah, maybe we have some future priests of Janus in the making!

C: 你是在捉蝴蝶吗？
E: Are you chasing butterflies?

C: 不过，再不去送信就要迟到了迷！
E: Still... I better go deliver that letter, or I'll be late, mem!

C: 只可惜盐粒过粗，火候也有所欠缺。汤浑浊不清，白白芋的口感也过硬了些迷。
E: But alas, the grains were too coarse, and the heat wasn't quite right. The soup turned murky, and the white taro came out a little too firm, memi.

C: 但我相信，随着再创世的降临，一切都将变得简洁有序，若虫们的行为也不会那么「BUG」了。
E: Still, I believe that with Era Nova's arrival, everything will become clean and orderly again. Even the nymphs will stop being so "buggy."

C: 趴在桌子上休息时，远远就瞟见{NICKNAME}过来啦，和记忆中一样从容不迫呢。
E: I was just resting on the table when I spotted {NICKNAME} coming from afar. Still so cool and composed, just like in my memories!

C: …呀，是{NICKNAME}！怎么不说一声呢，人家没做好准备……
E: ...Ah. It's you, {NICKNAME}! Why didn't you warn me? I wasn't ready...

C: 想做些有意义的事。
E: I want to do something meaningful.

C: 也许是亲密度进化。
E: Maybe it evolves through bonding.

C: 暂时不想做什么……
E: I don't feel like doing anything right now...

C: 阁下，你会怎、怎么描绘剑旗爵斩杀法吉娜的瞬间呢？
E: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, how... how would you depict the moment Dux Gladiorum struck down Phagousa?

C: 聊聊你的过去吧。
E: Let's talk about your past.

C: 我曾是布利萨的王储，我们的城邦信仰艾格勒。泰坦战争的时候，艾格勒和尼卡多利打仗，我们也不得不和悬锋城较上劲。
E: I was once the crown prince of Bulsa, and my city-state was loyal to Aquila. During the Titan War, as Aquila and Nikador came to blows, we found ourselves with little choice but to face Castrum Kremnos.

C: 下次见，贵客。
E: Next time, dear guest.

C: 我倒是无所谓，但维吉妮娅脸皮薄，遇到这样的时候总忍不住往我身后躲。
E: I don't really care, but Verginia's shy. She can't help but hide behind me when this happens.

C: 来，一起享受她的赐福…醉倒在欢宴中吧……
E: Come, partake of her grace... surrender to revelry...

C: 天外的「救世主」…您有何贵干？
E: Ah, the Deliverer from beyond the sky... What might I do for you?

C: 但无妨，这是我自己选择的路。虽不比那些天赋异能的黄金裔，行于阴影中的暗杀者也会证明其价值。
E: But it matters not, for I chose this path myself. While I cannot compare to those Chrysos Heirs with extraordinary talents, an assassin who lurks in the shadows can also prove their worth.

C: 再会，救世主。
E: Goodbye, Deliverer.

C: 吼！还好本狮不叫大壮。
E: Roar! Thankfully, my name is not "Bossman."

C: 提供「记忆残晶」。
E: Provide Memory Crystal Shards.

C: 站在你面前的，是妖精餐馆的终身会员——啦嘻嘻迷！
E: Standing before you now is a lifetime member of The Fairy Restaurant — Latitimem!

C: 我好像要失业了…
E: Am I getting laid off...?

C: 大姐，我是实诚人，卖东西童叟无欺！您看我这肥皂，用传说中的西尔菲翁和顶好的橄榄油手工做的，是黑潮前才有的货哩……
E: Madam, I'm a decent merchant who sells quality goods at honest prices! Check this soap. It's handmade with the legendary Silphion and top-notch olive oil. Usually you can only find such good stuff before the black tide...

C: …谢谢你。
E: ...Thank you.

C: 锻造有技巧…
E: There's a knack to forging...

C: 看来情况不怎么乐观…
E: Things aren't looking particularly optimistic at all...

C: 咕咕…噢！有个热心肠的孩子，总爱揽下所有接待新生的活…估计它在<color=#dbc291ff>学校大门口</color>忙着呢，去看看吧！
E: Hoot hoot... Oh! There's a warm-hearted kid, who loves to take on the responsibility of welcoming new students... I bet they're busy at the <color=#dbc291ff>academy's entrance</color>. Go take a look!

C: 对你而言，我的语言或许是噪音；但对于真正的迷途之人而言，我的声音就是夜海的港灯！
E: My words may be noise to you, but for people who are lost, my voice is like a lighthouse in the stormy seas!

C: 问得很好！只有理清了行动的本真理念，才会达成真正的互相理解。
E: Good question! Only by understanding the motives behind the actions can mutual understanding be truly achieved.

C: 而且现如今人力也还算充足。我还有一些老朋友可以为我提供援助。
E: We've got enough people. A few of my old friends are willing to help.

C: 老板发大财呢~
E: Boss is making it big~

C: …你都这样了，肯定能毕业。
E: ...With all that work you've put in, you'll definitely graduate.

C: 感觉世界末日的毕业没必要吧？
E: Is an apocalyptic graduation really necessary?

C: 又被做局了喀！
E: In a bind again, crrk!

C: 我虽已忘却过去，但在与你的交流中，我获得了崭新的记忆。
E: Though I have forgotten my past, in speaking with you, I have gained new memories.

C: 克拉特鲁斯爷爷！请再讲讲您和先王那场比拼吧！
E: Grandpa Krateros! Please tell us again about your showdown with the late king!

C: 小丫头，注意你的措辞！污蔑祭司可是大罪——
E: Watch your words, brat! Slandering a priest is a serious offense—

C: 问问大地兽，看它喜欢什么？
E: Ask the dromases what they like?

C: 错，不是卫兵，是厨子。
E: Wrong, not a guard, a cook.

C: 呜嗡！（他说他很感谢我，因为这件事让他懂得了「有时候就算说实话，大家也不会相信」这个道理。）
E: Grumph! (He said he was grateful to me, because this incident taught him that "sometimes even when you tell the truth, people won't believe you anyway.")

C: 裁缝铺三巨头，集合！
E: The Three Tailoring Titans, assemble!

C: 是啊，不过，一想到又要朝九晚五准点到岗，我就连尾巴都打蔫了。
E: Yeah, but just thinking about having to clock in nine-to-five makes even my tail droop.

C: 你是不是…曾经去过？
E: Have you... been there before?

C: 哎？竟然是……
E: Huh? It says...

C: 难道是白宝？
E: Then maybe Snowy?

C: 「后」是实力最强的棋子，来去如风，不可多得。希望你的旅程也能如它一般自由。
E: The "Queen" is the most powerful piece on the board. It moves like the wind and is truly one of a kind. I hope your journey can be just as free.

C: 送快递？就是把大家想要的东西送到他们手里吗？
E: Deliveries? Like, bringing things that people want to them?

C: 嘿嘿，*我们*希望能够像天上的星星一样，散布在每一个你失眠的夜晚。而现在，小灰也有一颗属于自己的星星啦！
E: Hehe, we hope to be like the stars in the sky, scattered across every sleepless night of yours. And now, you have a star of your very own, Little Gray!

C: 别争了，我们都是他的老师。
E: Stop arguing, we're all his teachers.

C: 我找齐了这里的所有若虫。
E: I've found all the nymphs here.

C: 你好啊，贵客。故事的终章已至，身为衣匠的我也总算能完成使命了。
E: Greetings, honored guest. The final chapter of this tale is upon us. At last, I, the Garmentmaker, may complete my mission.

C: 活物太过复杂，不可能带走，但几件纪念品应该不成问题…或许，这才是阿格莱雅大人一直寻找的、真正战胜黑潮的方式。
E: Living beings are too complex to take away, but a few souvenirs shouldn't be a problem... Perhaps this is what Lady Aglaea has been searching for all along, the true way to defeat the black tide.

C: 再会了。
E: Farewell.

C: 现在他是车上的智库维护者和生物学家，但很奇怪，他那么喜欢读书，却不怎么愿意和黑塔女士交流。
E: Now he is the biologist and overseer of the data bank on the Express. It's strange... With how much he likes to read, I'm surprised he doesn't like to talk with Madam Herta.

C: 不过和你一样，因为某些原因我最终还是离开了故乡，登上了这辆列车。随着旅程展开，过去战斗时热血沸腾的感觉也渐渐回来了……
E: But, just like you, I left my home for my own reasons and boarded this Express. As the journey unfolds, that blood-boiling feeling from the heat of the battle gradually returned to me...

C: （三把兵器便是一座岛屿，这里总共是四柄铳枪，并不是这里…）
E: (Three weapons make an island. There are four guns here. Not here...)

C: 我收集齐了《冒险鼹鼠队》…
E: I collected all of The Adventurous Moles...

C: （既然在这里捡到了缺失的书页，那么谜题就在前面咖啡馆里吧…）
E: (Since I found the missing page here, the riddle should be in the café up ahead...)

C: 太难了呗，这咋操作嘛…看不太懂。
E: Like I said, it's too hard. How should I go about it... I don't understand.

C: 下次再来。我要拿出杀手锏了，看我给你准备一道终极难题……
E: Do come back soon, and I'll show you my trump card. Let's see you try to solve my ultimate puzzle...

C: 不简单…真不简单。你做矿车工的天赋绝对是一流的。
E: Outstanding... Your gift is really hard to come by. Your skills as a mine cart operator really are first class.

C: 师傅说，矿车工是矿山的血液，是咱保证了地髓能够源源不断从矿道深处运输出来。
E: My master said that the mine cart operator is the lifeblood of the mine. The operator ensures the Geomarrow can be continuously transported out of the depths of the mine.

C: 滴、答，不要跟我说话，不要跟我说话……
E: Tick, tock, don't talk to me, don't talk to me...

C: 滴答？我好像想起来了…我是扮演钟表小子的迷钟……
E: Tick-tock? I think I remember... I'm the Ticker that plays Clockie...

C: 呵呵，因为我醒悟了，我不会再说这种可笑的话了。
E: Heh-heh, it's because I've been enlightened. I won't be saying any silly catchphrases like that again.

C: …你不觉得一般的客房都太无聊了吗？床单要熨得整整齐齐，用具都要摆好，太死板了，还是来点刺激的吧！
E: ...Don't you feel that most guest rooms are too mundane? It's so dull to have neatly ironed bedsheets and properly arranged items. Isn't it better to have some excitement?

C: 果然，我就知道！像您这样的专家，通过这种程度的挑战肯定不在话下！
E: Aha, I knew it! Clearing a stage is a piece of cake for a pro like you!

C: 但现在后悔也无济于事…唉，还是先继续解决问题吧……
E: But it's no use regretting things now... Ack, let's get back to solving the problem at hand.

C: 是啊，言外之意不就是让我自觉滚蛋吗？全完了全完了…这下得罪了最不该最得罪的人，我别想在这里继续待下去了啊……
E: Yup, isn't the implication for me to beat it? It's over, it's all over... I've offended the last person that should ever be offended. There is no way I could ever keep on staying here like this...

C: （按下回车键。）
E: (Press Enter.)

C: 嗯…嗯嗯…《晨曦》——不错不错，这可是个好名字。
E: Mmmm... Mmm-hmm... Ray of Dawn — very nice. It's a great name.

C: 任务很简单：你只要带着这台记录仪器前往下层区，与<color=#dbc291ff>「自动机兵•蜘蛛」</color>战斗一次就好。
E: This is a simple job: Just take this recording device down to the Underworld and get into a fight with an <color=#dbc291ff>Automaton Spider</color> — that's all.

C: 怎么样，要比划比划吗？还是说，你被我的大宝贝吓怕了？
E: How about it, then? A fight? Or are you scared of my baby?

C: 求你帮我个忙吧——如果能看到你战斗的英姿，我应该会对这这件武器有更深刻的理解。这样一来，我做出来的展品模型就是有灵魂的！
E: Please, can you do me a favor? If I can see your dashing figure in a real fight, it'll give me a better understanding of this weapon. That way, I can breathe a soul into the model!

C: 我可以开始战斗表演了。
E: I'm ready to give a combat demonstration.

C: 多亏了你啊，{NICKNAME}——你是我们贝洛伯格真正的英雄，当之无愧。
E: We owe this to you, {NICKNAME}. You are the true Hero of Belobog. You deserve that title.

C: 「约翰•匹克之选」牌缝纫机的广告画，但是复制品。
E: An advertisement illustration of a John Peak's Choice brand sewing machine, but this is a replica.

C: 现已停产的某型号显示屏，陈年古董。
E: A display screen of a certain model that has been discontinued. An antique.

C: 解说词到此结束了。
E: The narration ends.

C: 胆子大的监船工人登上星槎查看，发现船上空无一人，只有呜呜哀嚎的学舌妖，走向深处后，他发现船上货物满载，甚至还有散发着热气的食物。
E: The more courageous worker boarded the starskiff to take a look, only to discover that there was no one on the skiff. There was only a wailing Parrot Goblin. As he walked deeper, he found the skiff full of cargo, and even contained food that was still warm.

C: 恐怖片里最忌讳分头行动了。
E: Splitting up is taboo in horror movies.

C: （难道说，那就是传闻中的幽灵星槎？）
E: (Could that be the legendary phantom starskiff?)

C: 那个从来都很果决硬气的妈妈，怎么会变成这样呢？所以我搜集了几条关于这艘星槎的传言，来到这儿，想探个究竟。
E: Why would someone as unwavering and firm like my Mom turn out like this? So, I've researched several rumors about this starskiff, and here I am to investigate it further.

C: 驭、驭空大人，我是十王司的判官…那个，我担心您已受到岁阳侵扰……
E: Ma-Madam Yukong, I'm a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission... Well, I'm afraid that you have been affected by a heliobus...

C: 那是…幽灵星槎？！
E: Is that... the phantom starskiff!?

C: 你是需要遥控人偶吧，说清楚好吗？
E: You need some remote-control puppets, right? Can't you just say so?

C: 直接拍照。
E: Take a pic.

C: 这豆汁儿啊，要尝它的「酸」，酸中带…怪的味道。
E: I'd describe the taste as... unique. And a little sour.

C: 我是不是在哪里见过你啊？
E: Have I seen you somewhere before?

C: 在这里围观的有一个算一个，谁不知道金人巷商会管理无方、经营无能。如今这码头租金积欠不还，你说这得怪谁？
E: Everyone here knows that the Aurum Alley Merchant Guild is as incompetent as it is dysfunctional. The lease for the docks isn't even paid yet — anyone care to guess whose fault that is...?

C: …冷、冷静下来，才发现要是输了会给云骑军丢脸。但是你瞧那家伙气焰嚣张的样子，他要是败了学狗叫，那狼狈样子一定很有趣。
E: ...Now that I think about it, if I lose the bet, it'll reflect badly on the Cloud Knights... but you can't give in to bullies! He's the one who'll be barking like a dog.

C: 建木灾变后，玉兆的运筹功能都被限定在特殊部门的工作上。区区小巷子里的商业规划，只能靠咱们的脑瓜了。
E: After the Ambrosial Arbor disaster, the use of jade abaci for any kind of strategic planning was restricted to certain departments. For business planning in Aurum Alley, we can only rely on our brains.

C: 道歉的事倒是不打紧。但我真心想帮助金人巷。
E: I'm not worried about that, I just want to help save Aurum Alley.

C: 如果是「急人所急」之事，我也能帮忙。
E: If it involves helping people in need, count me in too.

C: 你们要记住，就算你们取代了我，危机还是迫在眉睫——你们至少得挣到「投标保证金」的数额，才有机会挫败公司。
E: You have to remember that even when you're acting in my place, a crisis is still imminent — you must earn at least the amount of the bidding deposit to even have a chance of thwarting off the IPC.

C: 唉，想问什么就问吧。
E: *sigh* Ask what you wanted to ask.

C: 不过呢，我不会后悔的。好日子过去了。长生种得学会接受事实，接受短生种们改造自己的土地，把夜市变成货运码头……
E: Not the easiest decision to make, but I won't regret it. The good days are behind us — we must accept that short-life species will repurpose our lands, and that night markets are destined to become shipping docks...

C: 金人巷历史交易量？
E: The historical trading volume of the Alley?

C: 我早就猜到你是公司的人。
E: Can't say I'm surprised.

C: 遇到迷路的孩子请联系地衡司。
E: Please Report All Lost Children to the Realm-Keeping Commission

C: 这句话可以一直用下去。
E: Very original.

C: 我们付出了很多其他的努力！
E: We did much more than simply utilize the dock!

C: 出示
E: (Present evidence)

C: 人们会始终需要金人巷美食街。
E: When people think of Aurum Alley, they think about its food. The two notions can't be separated.

C: 对，{F#她}{M#他}从不会客气的。
E: {F#She}{M#He} meant to say "thank you very much."

C: 不好意思呀，街坊们都说我说话直，你也别往心里去。
E: Sorry. The neighbors all say I'm too blunt. Please don't take it to heart.

C: 金人巷码头运输效率低下 、成交量低，根本没有创造价值，这就是经营不善的证据。
E: The inefficient shipping and low volume of transactions at the Aurum Alley dock — which does not create any value at all — are clear evidence of poor management.

C: 不降反升，这不可能…难道你们在虚张声势？
E: The shop does better than it used to? Impossible... you must be bluffing.

C: 别问，公务。
E: No questions. Official business.

C: 可恶，我为什么要回答你，我现在就要让你伏法！
E: Listen, I don't owe you any answers, because I'm gonna execute you — right here, right now!

C: 金人巷复兴计划还没完成，我们继续加油吧。
E: There's still more work to do to revitalize Aurum Alley. Let's give it our all.

C: 鹤运物流反馈说，物流规划还没有完成…要不要再确认一下？
E: Heron Express said that the logistics plans aren't complete yet... Should we double-check them?

C: 守灵不是出来遛弯的。
E: Shouling isn't out for a walk.

C: 这次拜托大人回收的是那些<color=#dbc291ff>异色的「逸散岁阳」</color>，绝非是随处可见的岁阳。大人操作「降魔机巧鸟」后一眼便知。
E: What we would like to ask you to retrieve this time are the <color=#dbc291ff>dispersed heliobi of different colors</color> this time. These aren't the heliobi you can see anywhere. You will instantly understand when you start to control the exorcist cycranes.

C: 「斗技表演赛」又是什么？
E: What's the "Martial Exhibition" all about?

C: 总之，我会给根据感应仪指向的方位，给你一个大概的坐标。而你需要去那里帮我看看，有没有什么异常情况。
E: To make long story short, I will give you a direction based on the general location the sensor is pinging. And you just need to go to the spot and look around for anything abnormal.

C: 一个线索不会落下。
E: I won't miss out anything.

C: 柜子被锁了起来，需要找到「钥匙」才可以打开这扇门。
E: The wardrobe is locked. I need the "key" to open it.

C: 你隐隐约约感受到身旁有路过的人在偷偷地看你，可当你想要用眼神捕捉时，对方却又快速而胆怯地消失了……
E: You feel like people who are passing by were giving you sneaky looks, but when you try to spot who those people are, they slipped out of sight...

C: 总觉得这个故事在哪听过？
E: I feel like I've heard this story somewhere before.

C: 留下看守列车的丹恒
E: Dan Heng, who is guarding the Express

C: 当心，这片区域的障碍物比之前更多。
E: Watch out. There are more obstacles in this area than before.

C: 你——准确的说，梦泡的主要角色——揉着发痛的脑袋苏醒过来。
E: You — or more precisely — the protagonist of the dream bubble, wake up with a throbbing head.

C: 你和你的狱友小心翼翼地在监狱走廊上奔跑，躲过一队又一队警卫……
E: You and your cellmate stealthily run along the prison corridors, narrowly avoiding guard after guard...

C: 你短促地抬头望了一眼，那些聒噪的声画便再度出现了。你瞬间感到心烦无比，只觉得莫名地升腾起一肚子怒火——
E: You cast a fleeting glance at it, and soon the cacophonous scene returns, driving you up the wall...

C: 好啦，我猜你差不多也放松点了。特意请你过来，确实是有工作需要帮忙。
E: Alright, you seem more relaxed now. I invited you here because I needed your help.

C: 你抓住我了。对、对、对不起！因为我、我…不在那个地方哈哈哈！
E: You caught me! I—I'm sorry because I—I... am not there! Hahaha!

C: 我就是个乖孩子~收起那不羁的性子~过着幸福的日子~谁都喜欢骰子呼哈哈哈！
E: I'm a good boy~ I'm putting my uninhibited nature behind me~ It's all happy days from here~ Everyone likes dice, haha!

C: 唉，就很郁闷。为什么非要捉弄我呢，这世界上的伤心事还少吗？
E: It's so frustrating. Why do they have to make fun of me? Isn't there already enough sadness in the world?

C: 那就开始新的生活吧。
E: Embrace your new life, then.

C: 没有人会喜欢，我只是一个笑柄…好冷，我好孤独……
E: Nobody likes me. I'm just a standing joke... I'm feeling so cold, so lonely...

C: 汪！这是我们的酒吧，不准随便干坏事哇！
E: Woof! This is our lounge! We won't let any baddies do anything bad!

C: 哇嘎嘎嘎！小姐，你是瞧不起我们的演出水平嘛？来看我的专场，不会失望的！
E: Mwahahaha! Are you underestimating our performance skills, dear lady? Come to my show, and you won't be disappointed!

C: …调制结束了吗？
E: ...Is the mixing done?

C: 到底发生了什么？他必须给我一个满意的答案，这是他的责任。
E: What exactly happened? He has to give me an acceptable explanation. It's his responsibility.

C: 亲爱的朋友，你是来寻找宝藏的吧？
E: My dear friend, are you here for the treasure?

C: 真的吗？可不要在这种时候跟我开玩笑啊！
E: Really? Now isn't the time to joke around!

C: 我可没说我要干。
E: I didn't say I wanted the job.

C: 真的吗？家族的人也是这么说的，我还以为他们是在忽悠我……
E: Really? The Family people said the same thing, but I thought they were just trying to fool me...

C: 对了，最近匹诺康尼商业街的客户看中了我们的影片，提议进行广告合作，这肯定能给我们带来更多收益！
E: By the way, some clients in Penacony's shopping district have shown interest in our film, and want to collaborate with us to produce some advertisements. This will definitely bring us more revenue!

C: 确实全靠我。
E: Indeed, I made it happen.

C: 我是来找动画胶卷的。
E: I'm looking for an animation film reel.

C: 观看钟表小子的影迷成千上万，又有几人能看出它趣味之下的内涵与隐喻，又能有几人是为了这些东西才进入影院的呢？
E: Clockie has tons of fans, but how many of them can perceive the underlying messages and metaphors under the fun façade? And how many of them go to the cinemas for those things?

C: 那些回忆本该被遗忘，但既然想起来了，不妨和你说说吧，毕竟他也是你们「无名客」的前辈。
E: Those memories are supposed to fade away, but since they've now come to mind, I might as well share them with you. After all, he's your Nameless senior.

C: 贵客，我要先回「故乡」寻找这个答案，你也可以一起前来…如果你了解「钟表匠」，肯定知道那是哪里——通过「梦中不可能之事」到达的城镇。
E: I have to make a trip back to my homeland to search for the answer, and you are welcome to tag along... If you know the Watchmaker well, you'll surely know where I'm referring to... It's a place we can only access by doing something impossible in Dreamscape.

C: 呵呵，没什么，总之她是个讲道理的人，放心吧。
E: Heh, it's nothing. She's a reasonable person, so don't worry about it.

C: 原来如此，《钟表小子》的背后原来有这么多故事…「钟表匠」先生，你真了不起……
E: I see. There are so many stories behind the Clockie... Mr. Watchmaker, you're a great man...

C: 导演，最后的胶卷已经准备好了，就等你们来进行最后的剪辑了。
E: Director, the last film reel is ready. You can now proceed with the final editing.

C: 哈哈，相信导演你也明白——对我们来说，斗争，不如共赢。
E: Haha, as I'm sure you know, Director, a mutual victory is much more pragmatic than competition to us.

C: 有点意思，开拍！
E: Interesting! Action!

C: 你得<color=#dbc291ff>用镜头拍摄指定演员</color>，不能多也不能少。
E: You have to <color=#dbc291ff>use the camera to shoot specific actors</color> — no more, no less.

C: 就这样吧。
E: Let's call it a day.

C: 「垃垃斯基想出计策，钟表小子负责执行。气泡锂犬挡在同伴身前保护大家，而圆幕先生因此落下感动的泪水…他们战胜一个又一个敌人，向着远方的大树前进。」
E: "Lalasky comes up with a plan, and Clockie is charged with executing it. Bubble Hound takes point to protect everyone, causing Mr. Domescreen to be moved to tears... They defeat one enemy after another, edging closer to the faraway Great Tree."

C: 哼，机会错过可就没有第二次了。
E: Hmph, such opportunities don't come knocking twice.

C: 那是很久前，我与哈努兄弟并肩作战的时候……
E: That was a long time ago, when Brother Hanu and I fought side by side...

C: 这个钟表小子只能重复固定的台词，应该是鸢尾花制造的*道具*。
E: This Clockie can only repeat fixed lines. It might be a *prop* made by the Iris Family.

C: 「汽水先生的故事要从钟表小子造访之前讲起。那时的美梦小镇还被称为『噩梦小镇』，镇民们被石头老板所奴役，而汽水先生是这里的一个厨师 / 青春偶像 / 人造兵器「苏乐达•猎梦者X」。」
E: "Mr. Soda's story starts before Clockie arrived. Back then, Dreamville was still known as 'Nightmareville,' and Boss Stone ruled the townsfolk with an iron fist. Here we find Mr. Soda, who was a local chef / youthful idol / artificial weapon "SoulGlad: Dreamhunter X"."

C: 苏萨先生是苏乐达的创始人，据苏乐达企业所说，是他在监狱中发明了美味的苏乐达，用甜美安抚犯人们的痛苦。
E: Mr. Sousa was the founder of SoulGlad who, according to SoulGlad Enterprise's statements, invented the refreshing taste of SoulGlad while in prison to sweeten the bitter pain of the prisoners.

C: 行了，咱也别多说了，马上开始吧——
E: Fine, let's say no more and just get started right now...

C: 「嘿，亲爱的观众，这次故事的主角是我们熟知的超级大坏蛋——石头老板！很久以前，他奴役着『噩梦小镇』的镇民们，在镇中无恶不作，只为得到宝贵的石头 / 美丽的垃圾 / 圆满的爱情。」
E: "Hey there, viewers. The protagonist of this story is the one big bad egg who we're all very familiar with: Boss Stone! A long time ago, he subjugated the townsfolk of Nightmareville and did all kinds of wicked deeds — all so he could get precious stone / beautiful trash / wholesome love."

C: 好，那我就按照你选择的方案创作哈努兄弟的外传了！
E: Alright then, I'll write this Brother Hanu extra story based on the idea you picked!

C: 导演大人，谢谢你给我讲述这些事情，他们的故事不该被人忘记…我一定会用这部外传记录下他们的故事！
E: Director, thank you for telling me all that. Their stories should not be forgotten... I'm going to use this extra story to record their biographies!

C: 啾？真的吗？那「钟表匠」先生为什么要把他们放在这么重要的位置呢？
E: Tweet? Really? Then why would Mr. Watchmaker place them in such positions of importance?

C: 咳，比起其他的角色，他确实有点不可爱……
E: *cough* Compared to other characters, he's not very cute...

C: 不如让家族收购了。
E: Might as well let The Family buy it.

C: 出乎意料的结局…
E: Well, that ending was straight out of left field...

C: 故事向近期在银河间颇有名气的某位球棍使用者*致敬*。取材于现实，再加一点魔幻、一点荒诞…
E: The story pays homage to a baseball bat user who recently became famous throughout the cosmos. It's based on reality, with a pinch of fantasy, and just a hint of absurdity thrown in...

C: 这是个好问题！我们曾试着在某位赞助商提供的梦泡上开发，但后来——哦，那枚梦泡里的内容有点太过刺激了，不太适合向大众推广。
E: Great question! We tried developing it on a dream bubble supplied by a sponsor, but... *sigh* the content on that dream bubble was a little bit too exciting to be made available to the public.

C: 你描述了一个平平淡淡的暖心故事。
E: You tell an ordinary but heartwarming story.

C: 我怎么也没有想到，一个如此悲伤的场景，竟会被你们演绎得这么愉快和温馨。两人依然如同往时般友好，仿佛时间和地位对他们不值一提！
E: I never imagined that such a sad scene could be portrayed by you two in such a joyful and heartwarming way — two people sharing a long-cherished friendship, as if time and status meant nothing to them!

C: 「何物朝向死亡」
E: "Something Unto Death"

C: 回答：我不知道，也无需做出定论。
E: Answer: I don't know, and I don't need to provide a conclusion.

C: …我就是喜欢看这个嘛。
E: ...Yeah, I really like them, so what.

C: 哼，等你输了以后，你就知道本姑娘到底有什么本事了！
E: Hah! Once you bite the dust, you'll know what I'm capable of!

C: 就是就是，都是老熟人了。
E: Right! I bet you're quite familiar with them, too.

C: 尾巴大爷有什么好办法？
E: Do you have any ideas, Mr. Tail?

C: 这也算是「洗心革面」？
E: Is this what you call "changing his ways?"

C: 啊！斯科特专员，你别咬我！
E: Ah! Don't bite me, Specialist Skott!

C: 啊…是{NICKNAME}，还有三月七小姐！
E: Ah... It's {NICKNAME}, and Miss March 7th too!

C: 毕竟人还活着，说是「僵尸」有点太不礼貌了。
E: The person's still alive, so it's not very nice to call them a zombie.

C: 嗯，本姑娘小心提防着呢！
E: Okay, I'm on high alert right now!

C: 胜败乃兵家常事，请女侠重新来过。
E: Winning and losing are all part of the game, I encourage you to try again young hero.

C: 哈哈哈哈哈！终于让我抓住了你的软肋！
E: Hahaha! Gotcha! Caught you off guard!

C: 啊…这人确实没救了。也许要换个思路对付他。
E: Ah... this man is truly hopeless. Maybe I need to try a different approach this time.

C: 那东西的装甲太厚了。
E: The mechs' armor is too thick.

C: 别可是了！快走吧，再不走你们自个儿的秘密也保不住了——
E: Why? Because if you don't leave now, you'll be socially obliterated, too!

C: …对啊。你不会真的以为我傻了吧唧的吧！
E: ...Yeah. You don't really think I'm dumb, do you?

C: 我听说庇尔波因特最低职级的员工，只要一想起公司的科技与财富，也会不由自主地挺起胸膛…但是你仔细想想，公司的一切成就和财富跟你有任何关系吗？
E: I heard even the lowest-ranking Pier Point folks feel proud of the IPC's success and wealth... But when you really think about it, how much does the IPC's success and wealth have to do with you?

C: 人家说了他是医士，不是患者。再说了，你也没有住院啊！
E: He already said he's a healer, not a patient. And you're not hospitalized!

C: 你将三月七与公司的好事之徒约战的事细细道来。
E: You elaborate on the matter of the upcoming duel between March 7th and that IPC busybody.

C: 食品保质期…你做的到底是药还是食物呀？
E: Shelf life... Are you making medicine or food?

C: 什么是手撕冰山雪莲？
E: What's Hand-Shredded Icy Lotus?

C: 不过…世界上真的没有那种灵丹妙药吗？
E: But... is there really no miracle elixir in this world?

C: 这个只是一点心理暗示罢了…我如此说了之后，三月小姐是不是感觉药膳更有效了？
E: This was simply a psychological suggestion... Did you feel that the medicine was more effective after what I said, Miss March?

C: 到这就可以了！这说明你没有什么异常。
E: Stop right there! This means you are completely normal.

C: 老夫曾经为了「阿休」的设计睡不着觉，现在却提不起劲…哈哈。
E: There was a time when I toiled day and night into crafting A-Xiu's blueprint. Yet now, nothing seems to ignite that same drive within me... Haha.

C: 免谈。
E: Out of the question.

C: …倒是很传统的故事。
E: ...It's quite a common story.

C: 咱们俩都是如假包换的「垃圾桶爱好者」。
E: You and I are both bona fide "trashcan enthusiasts."

C: 我来说明——我需要借用你与贝洛伯格的伙伴们结下的深厚友谊，毕竟我…我在这个世界没有什么朋友，哈哈哈！
E: Let me explain... I will need to borrow the power of your strong friendship with your Belobog friends. After all... I have no friends in this world, hahaha!

C: 一人一桶站在孤儿院门口的高台眺望远方，有说有笑。
E: You see a person and a trashcan standing on the platform in front of the orphanage, laughing and talking as they stare into the distance.

C: 贝洛伯格…喔！好像听小桂子提起过，但没啥印象了！那是什么地方啊？
E: Belobog... Oh! I think Lil Gui mentioned it before, but I can't seem to remember. What kind of place is it?

C: 那就谢谢这位不知年龄的热心小姐姐了…我们先回休息室了。
E: I appreciate it. Thank you, new friend whose age I won't try to estimate... We're heading back to the lounge.

C: 你们的什么圈子？
E: What circle?

C: 稍等，{NICKNAME}。其实近期，我们「寻圈」借着演武仪典的东风，也在办一场大型活动。
E: Hold on, {NICKNAME}. Recently, our "Pathfinder" circle has also been organizing a major event, taking advantage of the momentum from the Wardance.

C: 我没啥兴趣。
E: I'm not interested.

C: 那就让我们开始播放吧。
E: Let's play the recording then.

C: 演武仪典的参赛选手。
E: A Wardance contestant.

C: 那个！你们俩！一会儿还有别的事要办吧？扛着这只宝宝一定很不方便吧！对吧对吧？！
E: Oh! Oh! You two! You guys have something important to attend to later, right? It must be awfully tiring lugging this cutie-pie around, right? Right? Right!?

C: （卢卡，凡事得往好处想！这不正好能锻炼我问路的能力吗？就问问旁边这个小哥吧。）
E: (Isn't this a great opportunity to practice asking for directions? Let's ask this guy.)

C: 几位误会了！罗浮仙舟的确正在进行名为「演武仪典」的比武庆典。
E: You all misunderstand! The Xianzhou Luofu is indeed hosting a martial arts competition called the Wardance.

C: 而普通参赛者，则需要参加叩关赛，不断过关斩将，才能抵达「竞锋守擂」的擂台。
E: As for ordinary contestants, they will have to participate in knockout stages and rise through the ranks to reach the ultimate stage of the "Ringmaster's Challenge."

C: 你怎么不见了…我还没看到你变强之后的样子呢……
E: Where did you go...? I still haven't seen you grow up big and strong...

C: 卢卡选手！全银河都在等待你重归赛场！我们爱你！
E: Luka, the entire cosmos has been waiting for your return! We all love ya!

C: 你说的我都害羞了…输得那么惨，一点都不「美」吧。
E: You're making me blush... I lost so badly... It didn't really seem "beautiful" at all.

C: 哦对了，我听说演武仪典中还有另一位擅用「铁臂」的选手。如果你们能相遇、对决，那该是一场多么壮美的舞蹈啊！
E: By the way, I heard there's another Wardance contestant who's good at using an iron arm. If you two are matched as opponents, it will surely be a magnificent dance!

C: 两位代表贝洛伯格出战的选手，在参加「竞锋守擂」前的最后一位对手，竟然是彼此！真是命运弄人！
E: These two contestants from Belobog are each other's final hurdle before advancing to the "Ringmaster's Challenge"! Fate sure has a sense of humor!

C: 再稍等一下。
E: Please give me a moment.

C: 二位误会了，善逝只是我的同行旅伴，并非参赛选手。这一次代表丹轮寺登台的，唯有贫僧一人而已。
E: You are mistaken. Sugata is merely my travel companion and not a competing contestant. I am the only one humbly representing Claretwheel Temple on stage.

C: 奢摩小姐并没有说谎，对吗，智械先生？
E: Miss Samatha is not lying, is she, Mr. Intellitron?

C: 在杀戮之外，我们仍有选择的权力，丹轮寺的存在就是最好的证明。
E: More than just killing, we still have the power to make choices. The existence of Claretwheel Temple is a prime example of this.

C: 么么！么！么么！
E: Mehr mehr! Mehr! Mehr mehr!

C: 真的吗？让我试试！
E: Really? Lemme try!

C: …你不恭喜我吗？
E: ...You're not going to congratulate me too?

C: 我不方便评价。
E: It's not my place to comment.

C: 怎么还急了？
E: Why so heated?

C: 我也感觉卢卡选手没有发挥全部实力。
E: I also thought that Luka wasn't exerting his full power.

C: 演武仪典也要与时俱进嘛，不管用什么武器，比赛打得精彩最重要。
E: The Wardance also has to keep up with the times. The array of arsenal used doesn't matter. What matters is that the matches are exciting.

C: 你脑补的也太多了，其实我也没想明白卢卡为什么不躲一躲。
E: You're overthinking it. I've never really understood why Luka didn't evade.

C: 不过躲也没用吧，毕竟那是枪……
E: Though there probably wasn't much point in dodging. I mean, it was a gun after all...

C: 前排左手边第二位。
E: Front row, second from the left.

C: 心地善良的就是纯美的…
E: Compassion and kindness are a kind of beauty in itself...

C: 终于轮到我了！我是《周刊闻椿》的记者，请叫我「八卦记者」就行。
E: It's finally my turn! I'm a journalist from Weekly Wenchun. Please call me "Tabloid Journalist."

C: 前排左手边第二位。
E: Front row, second from the left.

C: 史瓦罗选手是瞒着我们报名的，赛前我都不知道对手是他……
E: Svarog registered for this tournament behind our backs. We didn't even know he was our next opponent...

C: 我没什么要解释的，清者自清，卢卡选手的实力有目共睹。
E: There's nothing to explain. Our consciences are clear as day, and Luka's prowess is evident to all.

C: 史瓦罗选手的力量、技巧、速度都不比卢卡选手差，只是缺个好教练。
E: Svarog's strength, skill, and speed are not inferior to Luka's. He's only missing a good coach.

C: 飞船拘禁区，距演武仪典开始还有3个系统日。
E: Spaceship's Restricted Zone, 3 System Days before the start of the Wardance.

C: 过家家的游戏，该结束了！
E: But it's time to end this game of playing house!

C: 你到底支持哪一边啦！
E: Whose side are you on?

C: 呃…另一位客人听完就沉默了…这广告词那么烂吗？我构思了一星期呢！
E: Hmph... The other guest also didn't say anything when I said that... Is this tagline so terrible? I thought about it for a week!

C: 好的。这个玩法其实很简单——「连点成线，连线成网，在大地上绘制出一副伟大的画卷！」
E: Okay. It's pretty simple actually. Connect the dots to make a line, connect the lines to make a web, and weave a majestic drawing across the land!

C: 你这形象经过人家授权了吗？
E: Did he even give you his image rights?

C: 游戏这条赛道你应该是没机会了。下次有机会比点别的吧。大胃王之类的？你也许能战胜我。
E: You probably don't have any chance to defeat me in games. Maybe we can compete in other things next time. What about stuff like competitive eating? Maybe you'd stand a chance.

C: 银狼小姐？你怎么回来了？
E: Miss Silver Wolf? Why are you back?

C: 和我说这些是想…
E: Why are you telling me all this...

C: 灰毛到此游，深藏功名不久留。
E: Gray-haired one here, taking names, kickin' butt, leaving before stuff gets drear.

C: 就叫这个吧！
E: Let this be it!

C: 至少比我强……
E: At least she's better than me...

C: 要是事先知道我就会阻止了…
E: I would have if I knew about it beforehand...

C: 来都来了！
E: I mean, you're already here, though!

C: 啊，我的心已被乱破小姐夺走，此生或许不再有悲喜。
E: Ah, my heart has already been kidnapped by Ms. Rappa. My life may never know tragedy nor joy ever again.

C: 大家都忙着筹办庆典，校园里到处都是好玩的摊位和活动，光是闲逛都觉得眼花缭乱了。自由、欢快，这才是大学该有的氛围呀。
E: Everyone is busy preparing for the celebration, and the campus is filled with fun booths and activities. Just wandering around is enough to make you feel overwhelmed. Freedom and joy — this is what the atmosphere of a college should feel like.

C: 这是什么奇妙的比喻？
E: What sort of wonderful metaphor is that?

C: 哈哈哈，谁叫那个坏掉的机兵，吉他弹得比我还好呢？大自然还真是神奇啊。
E: Hahaha, who knew that broken automation can play the guitar better than I do? Nature is truly wondrous.

C: …是因为没人看演出，打算把票卖给熟人嘛？
E: ...Is it because no one wants to see it, that you're selling the tickets to your friends?

C: 你真相信她能赶回来？
E: Do you really think she'll make it back in time?

C: 哎呀，有点难说出口啊。
E: Ahh, I can't bring myself to say it.

C: 吉他•忍徒，阁下当真如此在意忍首的位置？
E: Ninja Initiate Guitar, do you really truly covet the title of Head Ninja?

C: 真的假的？
E: Are you serious?

C: 你不觉得玩乐队很开心吗？

E: Well, for example, I've seen a very special training method where you use a cardboard ninja helmet to cover your head to achieve maximum concentration.

E: You evidently knew that there had been an error and chose to not report it, taking part in the music party and using up space in the academy's quota. It will be difficult to make a case for your innocence. Before we reach a final decision, your student privileges will be canceled.

E: Either way, I was wrong to categorize music as "good" or "bad." I will continue to take part in band events. Please rest assured.

E: Ahem. Fellow ninja... We are Dazzling Band / Dazzling Collective / Dazzling Airship / Dazzling Initiate Hotel / Dazzling School Party!

E: ...Hang on. Has this band been firing members recently? Why is it always missing a member when it goes on stage?

C: 哼，你是抱着多大的觉悟说出这种话。匹诺康尼正值经济复苏的关键时期，任何差错都可能酿成大祸，你背负得起整个梦境的未来吗？
E: Hmph, do you have any understanding of what you've just said? Penacony is currently undergoing a critical economic recovery, and any mistake could be disastrous. Can you afford to be responsible for the future of the entire Dreamscape?

E: I probably can't leave, haha. And I'm just the former keyboardist — I haven't played in a band for a long time. Such youthful stuff should be left to you youngsters!

C: 好在，他们成功在货船被撑爆前抵达了目的地。丹恒在港口对那位满脸堆笑的学士饱以老拳。

E: Here's the thing — Being a famous director, Mr. Reca also has deep knowledge of photography. It's a pity we had to leave Paperfold University in a hurry, so I didn't get to pick his brain.

"Fountain."

E: "Stuck between a rock and a hard place, the realization suddenly dawned that this was still the Alchemy Commission: A paradise known for forging the elixir of life!"
